title: Fork Network Suspicious Activity
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:meta" event_type IN ("ForkEvent","WatchEvent")
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(actor_id) as unique_actors, count by repo_name, _time
| where unique_actors > 50 OR count > 200
| sort -count
description: Sudden stars/forks from new accounts or geo clusters can precede supply-chain attacks or leaked-token cloning.
value: Sudden stars/forks from new accounts or geo clusters can precede supply-chain attacks or leaked-token cloning.
implementation: Ingest public repo events for crown-jewel repositories. Correlate with token scope changes. Feed threat intel on abusive ASNs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub audit + Events API.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `fork`, `create` events; `WatchEvent` bursts.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest public repo events for crown-jewel repositories. Correlate with token scope changes. Feed threat intel on abusive ASNs.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:meta" event_type IN ("ForkEvent","WatchEvent")
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(actor_id) as unique_actors, count by repo_name, _time
| where unique_actors > 50 OR count > 200
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fork Network Suspicious Activity** ‚Äî Sudden stars/forks from new accounts or geo clusters can precede supply-chain attacks or leaked-token cloning.

Documented **Data sources**: `fork`, `create` events; `WatchEvent` bursts. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub audit + Events API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:meta. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:meta". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by repo_name, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where unique_actors > 50 OR count > 200` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (fork/star rate), Table (spikes), Geo map (actor country).
visualization: Line chart (fork/star rate), Table (spikes), Geo map (actor country).
